repo_prior_art.cite: none; closest catalogue entries are pid_dose_response_iv_v1, pid_ordinal_late_partial_defiers_v1, and pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v1, but none is an MTE/PRTE multi-IV endpoint-frontier proposal

published_prior_art.cite: MogstadSantosTorgovitsky2018 and HanYang2024 supply the LP endpoint machinery; the proposed W/kappa frontier reads like generic support-function and LP optimal-face sensitivity rather than a new econometric identification regime

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; the frontier statistic is currently a generic nested-polyhedron support drop/face-separation identity, so the draft needs a non-tautological econometric threshold beyond solving or restating the two LP endpoints.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace W_e,j as the endpoint drop itself with an independently computable certificate, for example a dual residual cone condition or closed-form rank test using only the row geometry and active coordinate duals before solving the saturated endpoint.
Add a smallest nontrivial K=2, J=2 worked example where the coordinate endpoint and saturated endpoint differ, with explicit M_j, M_joint, active faces, kappa, and endpoint gap.
Add a generic LP/convex-analysis prior-art paragraph explaining why the novelty is not support-function face separation per se, but the econometric specialization and any new computable threshold.

recommended_next_step: Revise Conjecture 1 so the flagship claim is a genuinely new, computable multi-IV/MTE adequacy threshold rather than the generic endpoint difference and optimal-face separation theorem.
